narrative.summary:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

5th Ave at 89th St — Frank Lloyd Wright's spiral, filled to the brim with Carol Bove's steel.

A 25-year retrospective of one of America's most esteemed sculptors, filling the entire six-story rotunda. Opened March 5, 2026.

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

5th Ave at 89th St — the inverted ziggurat itself.

You walk into Wright's spiral and immediately understand why they gave Bove the entire rotunda. Her steel sculptures — bent, crinkled, impossibly folded — inhabit the curving ramp like they grew there. This is a two-pronged affair: a 25-year survey of her work and a radical reimagining of the building itself. She's installed five lounges with tweedy wool cushions in galleries normally reserved for art. You're allowed — encouraged — to sit, rest, nap. The quarter-mile walk up the ramp has never felt like this.

blocks.title: The Exhibition
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗘𝗫𝗛𝗜𝗕𝗜𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡

Carol Bove is a sculptor who makes steel do things it shouldn't. Her pieces look soft — crumpled, bent, draped — but they're massive painted steel. The tension between what you see (fabric-like folds) and what you know (2,000 lbs of metal) is the whole point.

The retrospective spans her full career, from early assemblages to the monumental painted steel works she's known for. The Guggenheim's spiral ramp becomes part of the sculpture — you're walking through the work, not past it.

⭐ The lounges are a statement: Bove treated Wright's building as something that needed "home improvements, especially in the seating department"
⭐ Start at the top and walk down — the sculptures reveal themselves differently from above

blocks.title: Check
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗖𝗞

💰 Admission: $30 adults, $22 students/seniors

🌐 guggenheim.org
📞 (212) 423-3500

𝗢𝗡 𝗩𝗜𝗘𝗪: March 5, 2026 – ongoing

𝗛𝗢𝗨𝗥𝗦
Mon–Sun: 10:30 AM – 5:30 PM

𝗕𝗘𝗦𝗧 𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘𝗦
• Weekday morning (10:30am): Fewest crowds, best light in the rotunda
• Late afternoon: The light shifts beautifully through the skylight onto the steel
• Avoid: Weekend afternoons — the ramp gets congested

discovery.text: Discovered by Disco via neighbourhood research
